tool_input.plan:
# Refactor authentication to JWT-based session model

The current cookie-based session model is showing its age and we want to move to JWT before the mobile launch next quarter.

## Current state

Sessions are stored server-side in Redis with a 24-hour TTL. The session ID is sent in a httpOnly cookie. This is fine for the web app but the mobile team has been asking for a token-based flow.

### Threat model constraints

XSS exposure is currently mitigated by the httpOnly flag. Any JWT design must not regress this — storing tokens in localStorage is the obvious wrong move.

## Proposed approach

We will issue short-lived access tokens and longer-lived refresh tokens. Access tokens go in an httpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookie; refresh tokens rotate on every use.

### JWT structure

Standard claims plus a custom `tier` claim for plan-gating. Signed with RS256 against the existing key pair.

### Refresh rotation

Each refresh invalidates the previous token. A reuse-detection check forces full re-auth if an old refresh is presented after rotation.

# Migration path

Roll out behind a feature flag. Existing sessions remain valid until natural expiry; new logins use JWT.

## Order of operations

Auth endpoints first, then mobile-facing read endpoints, then write endpoints. We do not flip the flag globally until all three tiers are validated in staging.

# Risk assessment

Main risk is the refresh-rotation logic — bugs there cause user-visible logouts. We mitigate with extensive integration tests and a one-week staging soak before any production traffic.
